cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking when an ordinary tropical storm is called a hurricane. The options are A, B, C, D. Of these, only about half a dozen becomes the strong, circling winds of 75 miles per hour or more that are called hurricanes..." So here, the text mentions that when the winds reach 75 mph or more, it's called a hurricane. Looking at the options, option D is "When its winds reach 75 miles per hour." That seems to match directly. Let me check the other options to be sure. Option A says when it begins in the Atlantic and Caribbean seas. The text says that's where they're born, but not all storms there are hurricanes. Option B is when it hits the coast, but the text says several hurricanes make their way to the coast, not that they become hurricanes
